commands.review_summary:
The PR updates group auto-reply prompt guidance and related prompt tests/fixtures to permit `message(action=send)` with `presentation.blocks` inline buttons for live channel-native group decisions while keeping plain text on normal final replies.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main's automatic group prompt still blanket-bans same-group message tool sends while the message tool and Telegram paths support presentation-backed inline buttons. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer-supplied real Telegram proof, not a safe automated repair job.

target.title: fix(telegram): allow inline button sends in group prompts
target.body:
## Summary
- Updates group-chat auto-reply guidance so same-group plain-text sends still use the normal assistant reply, while live channel-native decisions can use `message` with `action=send` and `presentation.blocks` inline buttons.
- Adds the explicit silent-final-turn instruction after button sends to avoid duplicate delivery.
- Aligns both focused group prompt tests and the group intro prompt fixture.

## Why
The old prompt was a blanket same-group `message` tool ban. That accidentally discouraged valid Telegram inline-button sends even though the runtime already supports presentation-backed buttons in group/topic targets.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` — 5/5 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.trigger-handling.targets-active-session-native-stop.e2e.test.ts` — 20/20 passed

## Scope
Prompt/test only. No runtime delivery code, package metadata, config, generated dist, or installed OpenClaw files changed.
